biteOfDay: Pink worm under a float on Taneycomo early morning (before generation kicks in) — still the can't-miss play. On Table Rock, hit topwater at dawn then switch to dropshot or Carolina rig on main-lake points.

tableRock.clarity: Stained to clear; James River beginning to stain near Point 15

taneycomo.generation: Mornings off until ~1:00–2:00 PM, then ramps to ~12,800 cfs through afternoon

species.name: Bass
species.technique: Topwater at dawn, drop shot, Carolina rig, jigs on points near shad schools

species.name: Walleye
species.technique: Drop shot with live nightcrawler on extended main lake points and ledges

species.name: Crappie
species.technique: Brush/fallen timber near river channel, small jigs in white/chartreuse

species.name: Trout
species.technique: Pink worm under a float, jig under a float, drifting eggs, nightcrawlers, PowerBait

species.name: Bluegill
species.technique: Live nightcrawlers, small minnows near boat docks and standing timber

species.name: Catfish
species.technique: Cut bait (small bluegill preferred) near flat points, early morning or late evening

tip: Get out early — water is off on Taneycomo until 1-2 PM. Best fishing window is 5:30 AM – noon. Pink worm is the can't-miss bait right now. Light line (2 lb test) for spooky clear water. Check generation before wading — afternoon water comes up hard and fast.
